title: pellaea angulosa
intro: Pellaea angulosa, also known as the angled button fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. Pellaea angulosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or poultice, with the Maori people traditionally using it for its medicinal purposes.
